summary_one_line: Input must parse as valid SQL containing a bit-shift operator so the VDBE dispatches OP_ShiftLeft.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A syntactically valid SQL statement (a SELECT with an expression) that lexes a shift token (<< or >>), so the parser emits an OP_ShiftLeft/OP_ShiftRight VDBE opcode and the interpreter dispatches into the shift case at line 86940. This requires correct multi-byte SQL keyword and operator tokens, not just any bytes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal keyword 'SELECT' and carry repeated structured token runs ('+:L', '+#L'), i.e. a real SQL expression that reaches the VDBE; the winner takes the true branch (T=4) and only the winner enters the case body (line 86950 fires T=4 for W, T=0 for L).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short malformed fragments ('SELEEC .`', '""/"" ', '``g..;') that never lex into a SELECT with a shift expression; the loser's coverage shows T=0 at the OP_ShiftLeft case and the whole VDBE opcode dispatch row is T=0 F=40 for L versus T=4 for W.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operands move closer to matching (matching-prefix length for the string CMPs in SQL's keyword/token tables, Hamming-distance bucket for integer CMPs). This lets value_profile incrementally assemble the valid SELECT-with-shift syntax that the parser requires, whereas naive (and cmplog without value_profile) get no partial-match credit and stall on the all-or-nothing keyword/operator comparisons. Both decisive pairs add exactly value_profile, isolating CMP_MAP gradient as the enabling technique.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the OP_ShiftLeft VDBE opcode case, reached only when the SQL program contains a shift operation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: case OP_ShiftLeft:              /* same as TK_LSHIFT, in1, in2, out3 */

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs differ by exactly the value_profile technique.
The second decisive pair (value_profile_cmplog > cmplog) is also a value_profile delta.
Winner-resolving seeds begin with the literal SQL keyword SELECT and contain repeated structured token runs.
Loser-blocking seeds are short malformed fragments that do not form a valid SELECT statement.
Only the winner takes the true side of the OP_ShiftLeft case and enters the case body; the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L86940  T=4 F=315  T=0 F=40  case OP_ShiftLeft:              /* same as TK_LSHIFT, in1...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP rewards inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching via prefix-length / Hamming-distance buckets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The distance bucket encodes matching-prefix length for string/memory CMPs, the comparisons SQL's keyword/token tables use.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient, not I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not mandatory. Attribution rests on both decisive pairs being value_profile-only deltas (vp>naive and vpc>cmplog), which isolates value_profile's CMP_MAP prefix/Hamming-distance gradient as the enabling technique rather than I2S.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating OP_ShiftLeft behind valid SQL-keyword/operator string comparisons in which naive reaches the shift case as often as value_profile, showing the CMP_MAP prefix-distance gradient gives no advantage.

weakest_evidence_point: The seed-bisect winner seeds are tagged value_profile_cmplog rather than plain value_profile, so I cannot rule out an I2S contribution leaking through vpc into the same byte structure.
